title: Les verbes pronominaux — Reflexive verbs
objective: Describe your daily routine in detail using reflexive verbs (A2: can describe routines and habits).

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: the “self” pronoun comes before the verb
bridge.body: Reflexive verbs carry a pronoun that refers back to the subject: **je me lève** (I get [myself] up), **il se lave** (he washes [himself]). English often hides this (“I get up”), so don’t forget the **me / te / se / nous / vous / se** — and it goes **before** the verb. Think of Hindi’s खुद / अपने को. Order: subject + reflexive pronoun + verb.

example.french: Je me lève à six heures.

cards.englishGloss: to brush one's teeth (je me brosse les dents)

example.french: Je me brosse les dents après le petit-déjeuner.
example.englishGloss: I brush my teeth after breakfast.

cards.englishGloss: to go to bed (je me couche)

example.french: Je prends une douche le matin.
example.englishGloss: I take a shower in the morning.

exercises.prompt: Reflexive present: Je ___ à six heures. (get up — two words)

exercises.text: Le matin, il {{}} brosse les dents.

exercises.prompt: Which reflexive pronoun goes with « je »?
exercises.explanation: je → me, tu → te, il/elle → se, nous → nous, vous → vous, ils/elles → se.
